channel.title: Follow Builders — X/Twitter
channel.description: AI builders' latest tweets, curated by follow-builders

item.title: Swyx (@swyx): @_lopopolo @aiDotEngineer @badlogicgames Slow Down talk: https://t.co/w6H835zxQP
Swyx (@swyx): @_lopopolo @aiDotEngineer @badlogicgames Harnes Engineering talk: https://t.co/1SEZMSUBTc
Swyx (@swyx): in retrospect putting the slop cannons (@_lopopolo) on @aiDotEngineer talks day 1 and putting the gr...
item.description:
in retrospect putting the slop cannons (@_lopopolo) on @aiDotEngineer talks day 1 and putting the grown ups (@badlogicgames) on talks day 2 is working out pretty well for faithfully representing the most impt split in AI engineering right now https://t.co/ZR2l3aHzEH https://t.co/BhaUlCRtfp
Bio: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
affiliations: - @dxtipshq - @cognition - @temporalio - @aidotengineer - @latentspacepod ❤️ 32 🔁 5

See our blog post for videos and demos: https://t.co/YDE0XkFuUO
Request access to GPT-Rosalind here: https://t.co/XqrW4n4Lmg
Life Sciences plugin: https://t.co/m9JHIgLqNg
Bio: VP Science @OpenAI, BoD @Cisco @nature_org, LTC @USArmyReserve Ex: Pres @Planet, Head of Product @Instagram @Twitter ❤️ @elizabeth ultramarathons kids cats math ❤️ 48 🔁 2

item.title: Kevin Weil (@kevinweil): 💥 Super excited to launch GPT-Rosalind, our first frontier model built for scientific research acros...
item.description:
💥 Super excited to launch GPT-Rosalind, our first frontier model built for scientific research across biology, drug discovery, and translational medicine.
The model is trained in chemistry, protein engineering, genomics and more, including built-in knowledge of relevant databases and tools researchers use.
We want to make these capabilities available to scientists and researchers while maintaining strong safeguards against biological misuse. GPT-Rosalind is launching through a trusted access deployment structure for qualified customers.
We're also launching a Life Sciences plugin for Codex to everyone today, which works with our mainline models as well as GPT-Rosalind.
Bio: VP Science @OpenAI, BoD @Cisco @nature_org, LTC @USArmyReserve Ex: Pres @Planet, Head of Product @Instagram @Twitter ❤️ @elizabeth ultramarathons kids cats math ❤️ 772 🔁 56

item.title: Peter Yang (@petergyang): Claude and Codex apps should build an easy way to view and edit markdown files from web and mobile i...
item.description:
Claude and Codex apps should build an easy way to view and edit markdown files from web and mobile if I want to hand craft the last 10% of instructions
Bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 26 🔁 0

item.title: Peter Yang (@petergyang): When I build skills now I always ask AI to spin up a separate eval agent to do yes/no checks to grad...
item.description:
When I build skills now I always ask AI to spin up a separate eval agent to do yes/no checks to grade the first agent's output.
And if the output isn't "yes" across the board it asks the first agent to keep working.
Building one for YouTube thumbnails and titles now. https://t.co/UvOoxekYVy
Bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 15 🔁 0

item.title: Peter Yang (@petergyang): Why do any real work when you can just migrate from openclaw -> hermes -> perplexity computer ...
item.description:
Why do any real work when you can just migrate from openclaw -> hermes -> perplexity computer -> openclaw again
Bio: I share extremely practical AI tutorials and interviews | Join 140K+ readers at https://t.co/XYKTmGVH14 | Product at Roblox ❤️ 73 🔁 1

item.title: Cat Wu (@_catwu): We've set the default effort level for Opus 4.7 to xhigh in Claude Code. You can use /effort to adju...
item.description:
We've set the default effort level for Opus 4.7 to xhigh in Claude Code. You can use /effort to adjust this.
Excited for you to try Claude Code with Opus 4.7 and let us know your feedback!
Bio: claude code + cowork @anthropicai, prev: @dagster, @scale_ai ❤️ 61 🔁 1

item.title: Cat Wu (@_catwu): 3. Tell the model how to verify its changes. Put your testing workflow in your claude.md, or add a /...
item.description:
3. Tell the model how to verify its changes. Put your testing workflow in your claude.md, or add a /verify-app skill. Opus 4.7 is better at verifying it's work, and it's helpful to share any local dev tips that are hard to discover.
Bio: claude code + cowork @anthropicai, prev: @dagster, @scale_ai ❤️ 69 🔁 1

item.title:
Guillermo Rauch (@rauchg): The hardest thing about agents and backends is durability. @workflowsdk fixes this.
That LLM you're...
item.description:
The hardest thing about agents and backends is durability. @workflowsdk fixes this.
That LLM you're calling *will* go down. That service *will* rate limit you. That database *will* unexpectedly slow down. You *will* get paged 💀
I've been looking for a unicorn for a decade. I wanted the level of reliability of combining stuff like SQS / Kafka / microservices, and I absolutely did not want *that* at the same time 😂
Truly reliable systems like that are notoriously difficult to reason about, to develop locally, to test, to simulate, to deploy… Workflow SDK solves that without compromises.
We're doing what Next.js did for the frontend, but for one of the most important problems of the new generation of backend applications.
Notably, Workflow SDK has an incredible self-hosting and multi-cloud story from day 0. We've taken amazing lessons from Next.js and poured them into the many Worlds (adapters) you can deploy to.
Congrats to Pranay and the Workflow team on a generational ship: https://t.co/ub7vQ7L6yE
Bio: @vercel CEO ❤️ 405 🔁 32

item.title: Guillermo Rauch (@rauchg): The rate of progress in AI is relentless. You can capture the upside of its volatility with @aisdk a...
item.description:
The rate of progress in AI is relentless. You can capture the upside of its volatility with @aisdk and @vercel AI Gateway.
Congrats to @anthropicai on another banger ship, but @xai, @openai, and @googleai are coming. Gonna be a fun year. https://t.co/tRPh2lgL8S
Bio: @vercel CEO ❤️ 231 🔁 9

item.title: Alex Albert (@alexalbert__): Some of my favorite things in Opus 4.7: - Very good at async work and following instructions - Effor...
item.description:
Some of my favorite things in Opus 4.7: - Very good at async work and following instructions - Effort levels are far more predictable for token control (+ new xhigh level) - No more downscaling of high-res images - Noticeably more taste in UIs, slides, docs https://t.co/GlTiMfwFVa
Bio: Research @AnthropicAI. Opinions are my own! ❤️ 857 🔁 32

item.title: Aaron Levie (@levie): The new Codex is another jump in what agents will look like for knowledge workers. Agents that can c...
item.description:
The new Codex is another jump in what agents will look like for knowledge workers. Agents that can code, work with tools, and use computers, can begin to execute long running tasks in the background for all areas of work.
This can mean drafting reports, setting up data rooms for a merger, reviewing contracts, helping onboard clients, generating marketing assets, processing invoices, and more.
With the Box plugin inside of the new Codex, you can begin to automate almost any kind of work with enterprise content. And importantly, being able to work across multiple apps is a huge point of leverage because we can now far more easily connect our tools together.
Bio: ceo @box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI ❤️ 249 🔁 19

item.title:

Bio: Design @Cursor_ai. Early @NotionHQ, @Stripe, built startups. I make a world where anyone can make software. Aspiring k-pop idol. ❤️ 183 🔁 7

item.title:
Zara Zhang (@zarazhangrui): The 3 things that are the best use of my time:
1. Deep talk: having deep conversations/brainstorm s...
item.description:
The 3 things that are the best use of my time:
1. Deep talk: having deep conversations/brainstorm sessions with awesome people 2. Deep read: consume a piece of long-form content 3. Deep play: try crazy/random things with AI and see where it goes
Bio: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w ❤️ 85 🔁 2

Sam Altman (@sama): Codex can learn from experience and proactively suggest things it can do for you.
It now has an in-...
item.description:
Codex can learn from experience and proactively suggest things it can do for you.
It now has an in-app browser, many new plugins, and so much more.
Bio: AI is cool i guess ❤️ 1060 🔁 23

item.title:
Sam Altman (@sama): Lots of major improvements to Codex!
Computer use is a real update for me; it feels even more usefu...
item.description:
Lots of major improvements to Codex!
Computer use is a real update for me; it feels even more useful than I expected. It can use all of the apps on your Mac, in parallel and without interfering with your direct work.
Bio: AI is cool i guess ❤️ 7700 🔁 374

item.title: Claude (@claudeai): Claude Opus 4.7 is available today on https://t.co/tHPAZRgiuP, the Claude Platform, and all major cl...
item.description:
Claude Opus 4.7 is available today on https://t.co/tHPAZRgiuP, the Claude Platform, and all major cloud platforms.
Read more: https://t.co/FXsCSDFSv3
Bio: Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8d1e5 or download the app. ❤️ 3043 🔁 271

item.title: Claude (@claudeai): In Claude Code, the new /ultrareview command runs a dedicated review session that reads through your...
item.description:
In Claude Code, the new /ultrareview command runs a dedicated review session that reads through your changes and flags what a careful reviewer would catch.
We've also extended auto mode to Max users, so longer tasks run with fewer interruptions.
Bio: Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8d1e5 or download the app. ❤️ 2871 🔁 105

item.title: Claude (@claudeai): On the API, a new xhigh effort level between high and max gives you finer control over reasoning and...
item.description:
On the API, a new xhigh effort level between high and max gives you finer control over reasoning and latency on hard problems. Task budgets (beta) help Claude prioritize work and manage costs across longer runs.
Bio: Claude is an AI assistant built by @anthropicai to be safe, accurate, and secure. Talk to Claude on https://t.co/ZhTwG8d1e5 or download the app. ❤️ 2392 🔁 47
